title: festuca ovina var. pinifolia
intro: Festuca ovina var. pinifolia, also known as sheep's fescue or mountain fescue, is a variety of the Festuca ovina grass, a perennial plant native to mountainous regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Festuca ovina var. pinifolia can be made into infusions, teas, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine.
